data.title: VEON approves launch of initial $30M phase of share buyback program
data.description:
• VEON (NASDAQ:VEON) on Monday announced the commencement of the first phase of its share buyback program with respect to the company’s American Depositary Shares.
  • This first phase of the buyback will be in the amount of up to $30 million.
  • This $30 million first phase is part of VEON’s larger plan to execute a share buyback program of up to $100 million.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Fangdd Network prices $7M stock offering
data.description:
• Fangdd Network Group (NASDAQ:DUO) has priced an offering of US$7M of Class A ordinary shares, or pre-funded warrants in lieu thereof, at a price of US$0.7 a share.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering for general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about Dec. 10, 2024, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.
  • DUO shares were up 58% as of 1PM ET.

data.title: BQE Water to renew normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• BQE Water (TSXV:BQE:CA) said the TSX Venture Exchange has approved the firm's notice to to renew the normal course issuer bid.
  • Under the bid, the firm intends to buyback up to 64.1K shares.

data.title: CN reaches new tentative agreement with Unifor
data.description:
• CN said on Monday that it has reached a new tentative four-year collective agreement with Unifor. The union represents approximately 3,300 employees at CN in Canada, working in different mechanical, clerical, and intermodal functions.
  • No details of the tentative agreement will be released publicly until the agreement is ratified. The current collective agreement with Unifor expires on December 31, 2024.

data.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq decline from record highs, with Nvidia losing ground; Dow slips
data.description:
Major U.S. stock averages fell Monday, with market behemoth Nvidia (NVDA) emerging as a source of downward pressure after large-cap stocks marked record highs and gains for the previous week.

The S&P 500 (SP500) -0.4%, the Dow (DJI) -0.2%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.4%. The benchmark (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) logged record-closing peaks on Friday, aided by accelerating expectations for another rate cut by the Federal Reserve. The Dow last week marked its first-ever close above 45,000.

Investors came into Monday's session seeing a fresh Street-high 2025 target for the S&P 500 (SP500), with Oppenheimer initiating its view of 7,100. But on its way to locking in double-digit gains for 2024, nine of the S&P 500's (SP500) 11 sectors were in the red on Monday. Communication Services paced decliners by losing -1.1%, and Consumer Discretionary, last week's top-performing sector, slipped. Energy and Materials were the two advancers.

AI chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA), part of all three key indexes, was a notable decliner Monday, down -2.7%, but off session lows. China's antitrust agency reportedly opened an investigation into the second most-valuable publicly traded company on concerns it may have breached monopoly laws. The news was first reported domestically by Bloomberg.

Nvidia (NVDA) said in a statement that it "wins on merit," and that it was "happy to answer any questions regulators may have" about its business.

Meanwhile, separate deal-related developments for Interpublic Group of Companies (IPG) and Hershey (HSY) propelled shares in each to the top of the S&P 500 (SP500).

Hershey (HSY) +15% after Bloomberg reported that Mondelez (MDLZ) is evaluating a potential takeover of the chocolate maker. Mondelez (MDLZ) fell -2.2%. Interpublic (IPG) +10% after the company agreed to be purchased by rival advertising agency Omnicom (NYSE:OMC). Omnicom tumbled -8.3%.

Investors are keeping in sight consumer and wholesale inflation reports due later this week. The November consumer price index will land on Wednesday, while the producer price index will be released on the day after.

"A further surprise for 2025 would be a hike from the Federal Reserve and events like Wednesday’s U.S. CPI will be a key determinant even if the near-term implication will be whether the Fed cut next week or not," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. The Fed will meet on December 17-18.

In the fixed-income market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.19%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.13%.

In global news, the rule of the Assad family in Syria ended after 50 years as rebels took control of Damascus on Sunday.

data.title: Zentek regains Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• Zentek (NASDAQ:ZTEK) on Monday said it has received a Nasdaq notification, stating that the company has regained compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement rule of $1.00 per share for continued listing.
  • ZTEK is +2.94% to $1.1221.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Pan American Silver surges on mineral exploration program results
data.description:
Pan American Silver (NYSE:PAAS) +7.4% in Monday's trading after releasing new exploration drill results that discovered new deposits across its mining operations in Latin America and North America, which it said highlighted the potential for long-term organic growth at many of the company's assets.

Pan American (PAAS) said recent results for exploration programs at the Jacobina mine in Brazil, El Peñon in Chile, La Colorada in Mexico, Huaron in Peru, Minera Florida in Chile, and Timmins in Ontario, as well as its La Colorada Skarn project in Mexico, have been positive and expands the deposits it can tap into.

"Based on the results we were achieving in exploration this year, we increased our drill budget to over 450,000 meters for 2024," Emerson said.

data.title: AstraZeneca gets FDA breakthrough therapy status for Dato-DXd
data.description:
• AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) has received FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation for its drug Dato-DXd, also known as datopotamab deruxtecan, for the treatment of EGFR-mutated lung cancer.
  • The drug received the designation for locally advanced or metastatic EGFR-mutated non-small cell lung cancer with disease progression on or after treatment with an EGFR-tyrosine kinase inhibitor and platinum-based chemotherapy, according to a statement.
  • Dato-DXd, an antibody drug conjugate, is being jointly developed by AstraZeneca (AZN) and Daiichi Sankyo (OTCPK:DSKYF) (OTCPK:DSNKY).

data.title: Payoneer, Shift4 downgraded as KBW looks ahead to 2025 payments landscape
data.description:
Payoneer Global (NASDAQ:PAYO) shares slid 7.4% in Monday afternoon trading after Keefe, Bruyette & Woods downgraded the payments infrastructure platform to Market Perform from Outperform following the stock's 95% surge this year.

Analyst Sanjay Sakhrani noted that lower interest rates will likely limit the company's EBITDA margin expansion and EBITDA growth over the next couple of years. Another negative catalyst, he added, is "the threat of higher tariffs on China" -- which "could pose a risk given that roughly 35% of PAYO’s revenues are derived from [small businesses] in greater China."

Sakhrani also lowered rival Shift4 Payments (NYSE:FOUR) to Market Perform as a potential CEO transition could be an overhang on the stock over the near-term.

The analyst thinks Shift4's (NYSE:FOUR) new CEO will be current President Taylor Lauber, he wrote in a note, although it may take at least until the back half of next year for her to "settle in and earn investor confidence."

"To the extent the current CEO looks to be a seller of the company, we think a meaningful premium from current levels may be hard to attain on the back of an unsuccessful strategic sale process earlier this year as well as the increase in share price YTD."

(FOUR) shares edged down 0.2% at press time.

data.title: Vertex reports positive long-term data for Casgevy
data.description:
Vertex Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:VRTX) presented positive long-term data for its gene therapy Casgevy and issued a business update on the product.

The data showed 93% of treated patients, or 39 out of 42, with sickle cell disease who were at least 16 months of follow-up were free from vaso-occlusive crises, or VOCs, for at least 12 consecutive months. The mean duration of VOC-free was 30.9 months, with a maximum of 59.6 months, according to a statement.

For patients with transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia, or TDT, 98% of patients, or 53 out of 54, who were at least 16 months of follow-up achieved transfusion-independence for at least 12 consecutive months with a weighted average hemoglobin of at least 9 g/dL. The mean duration of transfusion independence was 34.5 months, with a maximum of 64.1 months.

The data was presented at the annual meeting of the American Society of Hematology, which is being held Dec. 7-10 in San Diego.

Vertex (VRTX) said that over 45 authorized treatment centers have been activated around the world to support the delivery of Casgevy, with more than 40 patients having had a first cell collection.

Vertex (VRTX) added it has received approval for a third manufacturing facility for the product with its partner Lonza (OTCPK:LZAGY) (OTCPK:LZAGF).

Casgevy was developed with CRISPR Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CRSP).
